creator: vivek@khera.org
text:
Thanks so much Matthias! This is a great solution for me.

I've configured my local poudriere box to build ARM6 packages so I can get a db6 version of netatalk3 built for my embedded device (also I build my packages without X11 which saves a ton of space).

FWIW, I found this ancient bug report in Debian where someone submitted gcc ASM code for db3 mutexes:

https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=291901;msg=7

I have no clue if that is useful here based on your comments.
